bot: HbA1c measures average blood glucose over 2-3 months; normal is below 5.7%. Higher values indicate prediabetes or diabetes risk. (Disclaimer: For medical advice, consult a qualified healthcare professional.)

Normal blood pressure is around 120/80 mmHg. Values above 130/80 are considered elevated, and you should monitor them with a doctor. (Disclaimer: For medical advice, consult a qualified healthcare professional.)
BMI is body mass index, a ratio of weight to height. 18.5-24.9 is normal, 25-29.9 is overweight, and 30+ is obese. (Disclaimer: For medical advice, consult a qualified healthcare professional.)

Normal blood pressure is around 120/80 mmHg. Values above 130/80 are considered elevated, and you should monitor them with a doctor. (Disclaimer: For medical advice, consult a qualified healthcare professional.)

Prevent heart disease with regular exercise, healthy diet, not smoking, maintaining healthy weight, and managing blood pressure, cholesterol, and diabetes. (Disclaimer: For medical advice, consult a qualified healthcare professional.)

Diabetes risk factors include high BMI, family history, poor diet, physical inactivity, high blood pressure, and high cholesterol. (Disclaimer: For medical advice, consult a qualified healthcare professional.)

If your blood sugar is high, stay hydrated, do light exercise, and follow your doctor’s guidance. Avoid sugary foods and monitor levels closely. (Disclaimer: For medical advice, consult a qualified healthcare professional.)
